Submodules#

netective.utils module#

netective.utils.compute_moments(data: np.ndarray, ddof: int = 1) → tuple[float, float, float, float]#

Computes the four first moments of a distribution.

Parameters:

  • data – An array containing the data points of the distribution.

  • ddof – The delta degrees of freedom. The divisor used in calculations is N - ddof,

  • 1 (where N represents the number of elements. By default ddof is) –

Returns:

A tuple containing the mean, variance, skewness, and kurtosis of the distribution.

Note

Uniform distributions have np.NAN as kurtosis and skewness. Nan will be propagated

netective.utils.concat_path(path, *paths)#
netective.utils.flatten_list_of_iterables(lst)#
netective.utils.parse_nets(paths: list[str], comments: str = '#', delimiter: str = '\t') → dict#

Reads network files and returns a dictionary of networkx.DiGraphs.

Firts column of the network file is considered as the source node and the second column is considered as the target node. The network file must be delimited by a tab character.

Parameters:

  • paths (list[str]) – List of paths to network files.

  • comments (str, optional) – Comment character. Defaults to ‘#’.

  • delimiter (str, optional) – Delimiter character. Defaults to ‘ ‘.

Returns:

Dictionary of networkx.DiGraphs.

Return type:

dict

Raises:

ValueError – If the network file is not a DiGraph.

netective.utils.run_parallel(f, my_iter, workers, verbose=False)#

Start the parallel processes.

Parameters:

  • f (function.) – Function to be executed in parallel.

  • my_iter (Iterable.) – Iterable with the inputs for f. Each element of iterable will be unzipped before calling f.

  • workers (Numer of processes to run in parallel.) –

Returns:

Results – Contains the results of the function f.

Return type:

zip object.

netective.utils.validate_network(G: nx.DiGraph | nx.Graph)#

Validates the network and returns a DiGraph or Graph object.
